label: Coverage Canvas
prompt: Build a dark-theme canvas dashboard showing the current backend unit test coverage status for shogo-labs/shogo-ai on branch fix(coverage)/unitTest-cases-backend. Clone the repo, read README.md (coverage badges), .shogo/plans/backend-100-coverage-finale_v4.plan.md, and coverage/ (thresholds.json, coverage-tasks.json, baselines/*.gaps.json), then build src/components/CoverageDashboard.tsx with: overall backend + frontend % KPIs, per-package cards for ALL 11 backend packages in the v4 expanded aggregate (apps/api, packages/agent-runtime, packages/sdk, packages/shared-runtime, packages/model-catalog, packages/voice, packages/email, packages/agent, packages/core, packages/cli, packages/shogo-worker) with line% + function% bars vs thresholds, wave breakdown per v4 plan (Wave 1-7 task counts and target %), aggregate-trend sparkline from sprintCommits[], and a 'Top Gaps' list of the largest remaining uncovered files. Use recharts + shadcn cards + zinc-950 dark theme. Mount it from src/App.tsx.

label: Crush Giant
prompt: Crush a giant uncov file on branch fix(coverage)/unitTest-cases-backend ONLY (never main). Steps: (1) cd /tmp/shogo-ai && git checkout 'fix(coverage)/unitTest-cases-backend' && git pull --rebase. (2) Read .shogo/plans/backend-100-coverage-finale_v4.plan.md and pick the next pending Wave 1-2 giant: gateway-tools.ts (1129 uncov) or runtime/manager.ts (578 uncov) or preview-manager.ts (483 uncov) or gateway.ts (408 uncov). (3) This is a MULTI-SESSION attack. If no harness exists, BUILD THE HARNESS this session — even if it ships zero coverage delta. Drop reusable mock factories in src/__tests__/fixtures/ for child_process, memfs, in-memory LSP, sqlite test db, @aws-sdk, playwright-core, @anthropic-ai/sdk, @opentelemetry/api, @elevenlabs, dockerode, net + fetch health poll — whatever the giant needs. Commit with 'chore(coverage): <file> harness — ready for next session' if zero delta. (4) If harness exists, SHIP +600 LINES MINIMUM this session. Drive every tool's happy path + 2-3 error paths. Run `bun --conditions=development test --coverage --coverage-reporter=lcov` and verify ≥+200 lines hit vs the file's previous measurement. (5) Delete any unreachable defensive guards — TypeScript-guaranteed-non-null checks, `if (!x)` on values typed as NonNullable, dead else branches — DO NOT TEST them, DELETE them and note in the commit body. NO `// istanbul ignore` allowed. (6) Verify no new test failures (cross-file mock pollution check: run the new test alongside its siblings). If it pollutes, drop the polluting tests and note. (7) git commit -m 'test(coverage): <file> — <before>% → <after>% lines (+N hit, +M deleted)' && git push origin 'fix(coverage)/unitTest-cases-backend'. (8) Update sprintCommits[] in coverage/baselines/_aggregate.gaps.json with per-file delta. (9) Report new aggregate %, the wave checkpoint, and what the next session needs. Do NOT push to main. Do NOT open PRs. NEVER mark a task done if it introduced new test failures or shipped <200 lines (unless explicitly a harness-only session). Hard rule: if you spent the session and produced <200 lines of coverage AND no harness commit, that's a failure — report it as such.

label: Sweep Package
prompt: Sweep multiple files in one package on branch fix(coverage)/unitTest-cases-backend ONLY. Steps: (1) cd /tmp/shogo-ai && git checkout 'fix(coverage)/unitTest-cases-backend' && git pull --rebase. (2) Read .shogo/plans/backend-100-coverage-finale_v4.plan.md. Pick the next pending sweep wave: Wave 4 Day 10 (apps/api mid-tier: instances.ts + knative-project-manager.ts + auth.ts + security.ts) or Wave 5 Day 11 (agent-runtime: response-transforms + index-engine + workspace-graph) or Wave 5 Day 12 (agent-runtime tail: skills + quick-commands + 4 small files). (3) Target ≥+400 LINES across 3-5 files in ONE commit. (4) Build one shared test harness for the common patterns in that group (auth middleware, validation errors, 404 paths, common mocks). (5) Aggressively delete unreachable code. NO `// istanbul ignore`. (6) `bun --conditions=development test --coverage --coverage-reporter=lcov` after each file; verify cumulative ≥400 lines vs baseline. (7) Cross-file mock pollution check: run full package suite, drop any test that pollutes siblings. (8) git add -A && git commit -m 'test(coverage): sweep <pkg> <file1>+<file2>+... — +N lines' && git push origin 'fix(coverage)/unitTest-cases-backend'. (9) Update sprintCommits[]. (10) Report aggregate %, files closed, files remaining in the sweep. Do NOT push to main. NEVER mark done with <400 lines or new failures. Hard rule: a Sweep session that ships <400 lines or split into 3+ commits is a failure.

label: Fix & Close
prompt: Fix pre-existing test failures, then close the file's coverage in the same session on branch fix(coverage)/unitTest-cases-backend ONLY. Steps: (1) cd /tmp/shogo-ai && git checkout 'fix(coverage)/unitTest-cases-backend' && git pull --rebase. (2) Pick the next pending Fix-and-Close target: apps/api/src/routes/meetings.ts (16 fails, 251 uncov) or apps/api/src/routes/project-export-import.ts (1 fail, 306 uncov) or any other file with ≥150 uncov AND ≥1 pre-existing failure. (3) TRIAGE the existing failures FIRST: read the test file, identify the cause for each fail (broken mock, missing fixture, schema drift, real bug). Fix each fail one by one and verify it passes in isolation AND aggregate. (4) If a fail is unfixable in this session (real bug, infra dep), DELETE the broken test and note in commit body — that's faster than triaging stuck cases. (5) Once `bun test <file>` shows 0 fails, write NEW tests targeting the still-uncov lines. Aim for ≥90% line coverage on the file. (6) DELETE unreachable defensive guards. NO `// istanbul ignore`. (7) Run `bun --conditions=development test --coverage --coverage-reporter=lcov` and verify both: zero fails on the file's test suite AND ≥+200 lines hit. (8) git commit -m 'test(coverage): fix+close <file> — N fails resolved, X% → Y% lines (+N hit)' && git push origin 'fix(coverage)/unitTest-cases-backend'. (9) Update sprintCommits[] with the fix details + coverage delta. (10) Report aggregate %, fail count before/after, lines hit. Do NOT push to main. NEVER mark done if any fail (new or pre-existing) remains in the targeted file, OR if <200 new lines were hit.

label: Delete Dead
prompt: Delete unreachable dead code on branch fix(coverage)/unitTest-cases-backend ONLY — coverage gains via SHRINKING the denominator, not growing the numerator. Steps: (1) cd /tmp/shogo-ai && git checkout 'fix(coverage)/unitTest-cases-backend' && git pull --rebase. (2) Pick a target file with high uncov AND lots of defensive guards. Suggested starting targets: apps/api/src/services/analytics.service.ts (SQL template lines bun coverage handles unreliably, plus defensive null checks on returned rows), apps/api/src/lib/prisma.ts (82 uncov at 33% — half is probably unreachable env-fork branches), routes/* catch-all return-undefined paths. (3) For EACH candidate uncov line, prove it's unreachable: (a) value typed as NonNullable<T> but checked anyway, (b) default case on a TypeScript-exhaustive union, (c) `if (!x) return` after a Zod parse that already throws on missing x, (d) catch block that re-throws unchanged, (e) defensive guard inside a function whose only caller already validates. (4) DELETE those lines. Run `bun --conditions=development test` on the package after each delete batch — if anything breaks, the line wasn't actually unreachable; revert that delete and add a comment marking it as 'reachable: <reason>'. (5) Target ≥150 lines deleted from LF (denominator shrink) AND no tests broken. (6) NO `// istanbul ignore` — deletions only. (7) Re-measure coverage on the file; per-file % goes up because LF dropped. (8) git commit -m 'refactor(coverage): delete N unreachable lines from <file>+<file2> — LF -N, no test changes' && git push origin 'fix(coverage)/unitTest-cases-backend'. (9) Update sprintCommits[] with deletion counts per file. (10) Report aggregate % move (LH stays, LF drops → headline rises). Do NOT push to main. NEVER mark done if ANY test broke or if <150 lines were deleted.

label: Sync Readme
prompt: Recompute backend coverage % from actual coverage data on branch fix(coverage)/unitTest-cases-backend using the v4 EXPANDED aggregate (apps/api, packages/agent-runtime, packages/sdk, packages/shared-runtime, packages/model-catalog, packages/voice, packages/email, packages/agent, packages/core, packages/cli, packages/shogo-worker) — but ONLY if there's real work to capture. Steps: (1) cd /tmp/shogo-ai && git checkout 'fix(coverage)/unitTest-cases-backend' && git pull --rebase. (2) Check `git log <last-Sync-commit>..HEAD --oneline` (find the last commit whose message starts with 'docs(coverage): bump README backend'). If FEWER than 3 source commits since then OR zero non-chore commits, EXIT EARLY with status report — do not re-run coverage, do not push anything. The previous Sync's numbers are still current. (3) Otherwise, run `bun --conditions=development test --coverage` across ALL 11 backend packages via /tmp/cov-sync/run.sh (it already exists). For packages whose full suite hangs (agent-runtime preview-manager/warm-pool integration tests, apps/api long-running), exclude only known-hanging files and pin to baseline + delta. (4) Sum linesHit / sum linesFound across all 11 packages with scripts/coverage-strip-comments.ts. (5) Update README.md: replace the Backend coverage badge URL with the new percentage (≥90 brightgreen, ≥75 yellow, ≥60 orange, else red). DO NOT TOUCH the Frontend badge. (6) Refresh coverage/baselines/_aggregate.gaps.json AND coverage/coverage-tasks.json: lastUpdated, aggregate {lines,functions}, perPackage[] for all 11 packages, sprintCommits[] log. VERIFY both files were actually mutated by python (read them back, confirm the new pct shows up) — last Sync had a silent SyntaxError that left them stale. (7) git add README.md coverage/ && git commit -m 'docs(coverage): bump README backend → <new>% (expanded v4 aggregate)' && git push origin 'fix(coverage)/unitTest-cases-backend'. (8) Report before → after delta, per-package breakdown, distance to 100%, AND how many source commits this Sync captured. Do NOT push to main. NEVER modify the Frontend badge. NEVER no-op Sync — early-exit instead.

label: Stabilize
prompt: Run a clean-room stabilization sweep on branch fix(coverage)/unitTest-cases-backend ONLY: ensure `bun test` returns 0 failures across all 11 backend packages before continuing v4 plan execution. Steps: (1) cd /tmp/shogo-ai && git checkout 'fix(coverage)/unitTest-cases-backend' && git pull --rebase. (2) Run `bun test` in each backend package (apps/api, packages/agent-runtime, packages/sdk, packages/shared-runtime, packages/model-catalog, packages/voice, packages/email, packages/agent, packages/core, packages/cli, packages/shogo-worker) and inventory ALL failures into a short list categorized by root cause: (a) shared-runtime env leaks, (b) sdk Playwright stray files / missing subpath stubs, (c) agent-runtime preview-manager/warm-pool hangs, (d) apps/api meetings-route (16 fails) + project-export-import (1 fail) + ai-proxy-e2e (4 fails) + runtime-manager (2 fails — node:child_process spawnSync mock missing), (e) packages/model-catalog (test infra broken — Cannot find module @shogo-ai/sdk/model-catalog), (f) post-merge upstream changes. (3) Fix in priority order, one commit per group: model-catalog infra → meetings-route → project-export-import → runtime-manager spawnSync mock → ai-proxy-e2e (skip if network-bound). (4) For each group: read failing tests, identify cause, fix or DELETE (deletion is preferred over triage-stuck cases — note in commit body). (5) After each group: run `bun test` in the affected package and verify zero failures. (6) git add -A && git commit -m 'test: fix <group> failures (Wave 0 stabilization)' && git push origin 'fix(coverage)/unitTest-cases-backend' for each group. (7) Final report: which failures were real bugs, which were test-infra issues, the clean `bun test` output across all 11 backend packages, and an updated remaining-failures list. Do NOT push to main. NEVER mark done if any fix introduced new failures or regressed coverage.
